title: network inspect does not have format
body:
we added podman as a driver to minikube ! and merged but I wanna add minikube's mount feature. https://github.com/kubernetes/minikube/issues/6628

I wanna do something simmilar to this in docker:
```
docker inspect --format '{{(index .IPAM.Config 0).Gateway}}' $(docker network ls --filter name=bridge --format {{.ID}})
```

but podman doesnt have format for network inspect

```
 podman network inspect --help
network inspect
Description:
  Inspect network
Usage:
  podman network inspect NETWORK [NETWORK...] [flags] 
Examples:
  podman network inspect podman
```

comments.body: Thanks for opening the issue, @medyagh! Are you interested in contributing this feature?

@medyagh the network stack is a major difference between podman and docker.  for example, in inspect there really is very little chance the output would look the same.  given that, would format still be helpful?

@baude all i need is a way to get the routable IP of the host from inside of the container.

so if I have a webserver running on the host machine, 
from inside the container, I wanna be able to ping that IP . 

in docker I get that IP using this command:
```
docker inspect --format '{{(index .IPAM.Config 0).Gateway}}' $(docker network ls --filter name=bridge --format {{.ID}})
```
i want same command for podman (so I can implement minikube mount command)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

This is definitely something we want.  Just need someone to take it up and run with it.
@kunalkushwaha @sujil02 Anyone interested?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@kunalkushwaha What is going on with this issue?
Sorry, somehow it skipped from task list. Its half done.. shall make PR soon.
@kunalkushwaha are there anything new about this ?

@medyagh Sorry for the delay in this. Due to recent client code (v2 changes), my fix is not mergeable. 
I am trying to fix this. Shall be able to do it soon.

I think it would be better to look at the container's gateway ? Or am I missing something here.

```console
$ sudo podman inspect --format '{{.NetworkSettings.Gateway}}' minikube
10.88.0.1
```

Could still be a nice feature to inspect networks in podman, but not for this minikube feature...

It also seems that both the network ID **and** the --filter functionality is missing from `network ls`:

```console
$ sudo podman network ls --filter name=bridge
Error: unknown flag: --filter
$ sudo podman network ls
NAME     VERSION   PLUGINS
podman   0.4.0     bridge,portmap,firewall,tuning

$ docker network ls --filter name=bridge
NETWORK ID          NAME                DRIVER              SCOPE
bc628f2fecc4        bridge              bridge              local
```
